question: What is the name of the indigenous Mahe-class Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft (ASW-SWC) recently commissioned by the Indian Navy?
detail:
- The Indian Navy will soon commission **INS Malvan**, the **second indigenous Mahe-class Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft (ASW-SWC)**.
- The **first** indigenous warship of the class is **INS Mahe**, commissioned into the Indian Navy on **24 November 2025** at the **Naval Dockyard, Mumbai**.
- Its purpose is to **detect and neutralise enemy Submarines and Underwater Drones** in coastal areas and to strengthen **Coastal Security**.
- The warship has been built indigenously by **Cochin Shipyard Limited (CSL), Kochi**, and developed with **80%+ Indigenous Content**.
- **What is an ASW-SWC:** a warship designed to detect, chase and — if needed — destroy enemy submarines in **shallow water** maritime zones.
- **Mahe-class:** a series (class) of the Indian Navy's new Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft. First ship — **INS Mahe (2025)**; second ship — **INS Malvan (2026)**.
- **About Cochin Shipyard Limited (CSL):** India's leading **Shipbuilding and Ship Repair PSU**. Established **1972**; headquarters **Kochi, Kerala**.

question: Where have India's first and deepest geothermal wells been commissioned?
detail:
- India commissioned its **first and deepest Geothermal Wells** on **17 July 2026** in the **Puga Valley, Ladakh**.
- The aim is to promote clean and **Renewable Energy** generation through **Geothermal Energy**.
- The project has been developed at Puga Valley, Ladakh by the **ONGC Energy Centre**.
- **Two Geothermal Wells of 1,000 metres depth** have been set up at an altitude of over **14,000 feet**.
- The wells have been connected to a **1 MW Pilot Geothermal Power Project**. During testing, a temperature of **135°C** was recorded at a depth of **400 metres**.
- **Geothermal Energy:** a Renewable Energy source based on the heat obtained from the **Earth's Interior**, used for **Electricity Generation** and **Direct Heating**.
- **Geothermal Wells:** deep wells dug to reach the **hot water or steam** present inside the earth; this hot steam turns a **Turbine** to produce electricity.

question: When is World Chess Day celebrated annually?
detail:
- **World Chess Day** is celebrated every year on **20 July**.
- The day is observed as the **foundation day of the International Chess Federation (FIDE)**, which was established on **20 July 1924**.
- The day was recognised by the **United Nations (UN)** in **2019**.
- **Theme 2026 — "Celebrate, create, and make a difference"**.
- **About FIDE:** the international body for chess; it organises chess competitions and gives rankings to chess players. Established **20 July 1924**; headquarters **Lausanne, Switzerland**; President **Arkady Dvorkovich**; Deputy President **Viswanathan Anand**.
- **Note:** FIDE has declared **2026** as the **Year of Chess in Education**.

question: Which drug has recently been approved by the FDA as the first oral PCSK9 inhibitor for treating high cholesterol?
detail:
- The **U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA)** approved **Lipfendra (Enlicitide)** as the **first Oral PCSK9 Inhibitor** for the treatment of **High Cholesterol (Hypercholesterolemia)**.
- Along with **diet and exercise**, the drug helps reduce the level of **LDL (Bad Cholesterol)**.
- Lipfendra is the **world's first Oral PCSK9 Inhibitor** — earlier PCSK9 Inhibitors were available only in **injectable** form.
- **What is High Cholesterol (Hypercholesterolemia):** when **LDL (Low-Density Lipoprotein)**, i.e. bad cholesterol, rises too much in the body, the condition is called Hypercholesterolemia.
- **About the FDA:** a government **Regulatory Agency of the USA** that ensures medicines, food products, vaccines and Medical Devices are **Safe, Effective and of good Quality**. It began in **1906** under the **Pure Food and Drugs Act**.

question: India's first private orbital rocket, Vikram-1, has been developed by which company?
detail:
- India's leading space startup **Skyroot Aerospace** successfully launched the country's first **Private Orbital Rocket 'Vikram-1'** on **18 July 2026**.
- The launch was carried out from the **Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC), Sriharikota**.
- With this successful launch, **Skyroot Aerospace became India's first private company to place a satellite into Earth's orbit**.
- **What is an Orbital Rocket:** a rocket that places a satellite or spacecraft into **Earth's Orbit** — that is, the object keeps revolving around the Earth and does not fall back.
- **Features of Vikram-1:** a **Multi-stage Orbital Launch Vehicle**; capable of placing about **290–350 kg payload** into a **500 km Low Earth Orbit (LEO)**; based on a **Carbon-composite Structure**; equipped with **3D-Printed Engines** and **Solid Fuel Boosters**.
- **History:** in **2022** Skyroot launched **Vikram-S**, a **sub-orbital rocket**.
- **Difference:** **Vikram-S** was only sub-orbital — it reached the boundary of space and fell back into the sea, never reaching orbit; **Vikram-1** is an **orbital mission** whose objective is to place a satellite into Earth's orbit.
- **About Skyroot Aerospace:** an Indian **Private Space Technology Company** that develops **Satellite Launch Vehicles**. Founded **2018**; headquarters **Hyderabad**.

question: Sir Garfield Sobers passed away recently. He was a great cricketer of which country?
detail:
- **Sir Garfield Sobers**, the great all-rounder of the **West Indies**, passed away at the age of **89**. He is counted among the **best all-rounders** in cricket history.
- In his honour, the **ICC** awards the **Sir Garfield Sobers Trophy** to the **Best Men's Cricketer** of the year.
- He played **93 Test matches** for the West Indies between **1954 and 1974**.
- In Test cricket he scored **8,032 runs** and took **235 wickets**.
- In **1958** he made **365 runs** against **Pakistan** — the **highest individual Test score** at that time.
- In **1968** he became the **first batsman to hit six sixes in an over** in first-class cricket.
- **Major Honours & Recognition:** **Wisden Cricketer of the Century (2000)** — selected among the Five Cricketers of the Century; **ICC Cricket Hall of Fame (2009)** — inaugural member; **Wisden Cricketer of the Year (1964)** and **Wisden Leading Cricketer in the World — 8 times**.
- **Other recent demise:** **Moozhikkal Pankajakshi**, the famous artist of **Nokkuvidya Pavakkali** and **Padma Shri awardee (2020)**, died on **15 July 2026** at the age of **90** in **Koothattukulam, Kerala**. She made a major contribution in giving Kerala's traditional puppetry art (Nokkuvidya Pavakkali) national and international recognition.

question: Which State topped the NITI Aayog Investment Friendliness Index 2026?
detail:
- **NITI Aayog** released the **Investment Friendliness Index 2026**, in which **Gujarat** stood **first**.
- The index ranks **28 States and 8 Union Territories** on the basis of their investment-friendly environment.
- It assesses the investment climate on parameters such as **Business Environment, Policies, Infrastructure, Ease of Doing Business, Regulatory System** and **Investment Support**.
- **Four categories based on Index Score:** **Top Performers** — more than 50 points; **Frontrunners** — 45 to 50 points; **Emerging Performers** — 40 to 45 points; **Aspiring States** — less than 40 points.
- **Top Rankings:** 1. **Gujarat (56.6)**, 2. **Maharashtra (53.7)**, 3. **Tamil Nadu (53.3)**. **Goa (53.1)** and **Odisha (52.4)** were also among the Top Performers.
- **About NITI Aayog:** the **Policy Think Tank** of the Government of India, which frames policy and gives strategic advice for the country's economic and social development. Established **1 January 2015**; Chairperson — the **Prime Minister of India**; CEO — **Nidhi Chhibber**.

question: Where was the 3rd India–European Union Trade and Technology Council (TTC) Meeting held?
detail:
- The **3rd India–EU Trade and Technology Council (TTC) Meeting** between India and the **European Union** was held in **Brussels, Belgium**.
- The objective of the meeting was to further strengthen cooperation in **Trade, Technology, Innovation and Resilient Supply Chains**, and to promote India–EU cooperation in emerging **Technologies, Business, Digital Transformation** and secure, strong **Supply Chains**.
- **About the TTC:** the India–EU Trade and Technology Council is a **High-Level Strategic Platform** set up to promote **Trade, Technology and Strategic Cooperation** between India and the European Union. Established **2022**.
- **About Belgium:** Capital — **Brussels**; Currency — **Euro (€)**; Prime Minister — **Bart De Wever**.

question: Who has been sworn in as the new Prime Minister of Lithuania?
detail:
- **Mindaugas Sinkevičius**, leader of the **Lithuanian Social Democratic Party (LSDP)**, has been sworn in as the **20th Prime Minister of Lithuania**.
- He replaced **Inga Ruginienė**, who **resigned on 14 July 2026**.
- **Who is Mindaugas Sinkevičius:** a senior politician of Lithuania who has earlier also served as **Minister of Economy and Innovation**, and has now taken charge as the 20th Prime Minister of Lithuania.
- **About Lithuania:** Capital — **Vilnius**; Currency — **Euro (€)**; President — **Gitanas Nausėda**.
- Lithuania is the **largest of the Baltic States (Lithuania, Latvia, Estonia)** in both **area and population**.

question: What is the main objective of Kerala Government's 'Operation Toofan'?
options: Interstate Drug Trafficking & Control of Narcotics Network
detail:
- **Operation Toofan** is a **multi-state anti-narcotics campaign** launched by the **Kerala Government**.
- Its objective is to establish effective control over **interstate drug trafficking, drug cartels and narcotics abuse**.
- It was launched by Kerala's **Home Minister Ramesh Chennithala**, while its **official inauguration** was done by **Chief Minister V. D. Satheesan**.
- **Five southern states** are part of the campaign — **Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana**.
- **Major Highlights:** the five southern states have agreed to create a **Permanent Joint Coordination Mechanism**; **Intelligence Sharing** and **Joint Operations** between the states will be strengthened; each state will appoint a **Nodal Officer of SP rank or above**; and joint action will be taken against drug smuggling through **Road, Rail, Air and Sea routes**.
